heading: Limitation on minimum wage
label: 6704 Limitation on minimum wage
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)670441:351(b)., , ; , , .Pub. L. 89–286, § 2(b)79 Stat. 1034Pub. L. 94–489, § 1(b)90 Stat. 2358Oct. 22, 1965Oct. 13, 1976
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)670441:351(b)., , ; , , .Pub. L. 89–286, § 2(b)79 Stat. 1034Pub. L. 94–489, § 1(b)90 Stat. 2358Oct. 22, 1965Oct. 13, 1976

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: A contractor that makes a contract with the Federal Government, the principal purpose of which is to furnish services through the use of service employees, and any subcontractor, may not pay less than the minimum wage specified under section 6(a)(1) of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 () to an employee engaged in performing work on the contract.29 U.S.C. 206(a)(1)

children.heading: .—Violations
children.label: b .—Violations
children.text: Sections 6705 to 6707(d) of this title are applicable to a violation of this section.
